title: Sadhukhan's Conjecture
statement_text: There are graphs with large girth that do not admit KT orientations.
context_text: Prior to this work, all known examples of graphs without a KT orientation contained cycles of length at most five. Sadhukhan conjectured that girth alone does not prevent the existence of graphs with no KT orientation, motivating the construction in Section 4 of the paper.
paper_contribution: Settles the conjecture affirmatively by constructing explicit graphs of arbitrarily large girth that admit no KT orientation.
notes: Stated in prose in the introduction with citation [sadhukhan2024shift]; no labelled theorem environment.
paper_title: Orientations of graphs with at most one directed path between every pair of vertices
